service_area_zips.note: Weekly mow routes through Cascades, Sugarland Run, and Countryside. Paver patios and re-leveling work on the older split-levels off Algonkian Pkwy.
Front-yard sod and seasonal mulch for the brick colonials near Folly Lick Stream. Tree and shrub maintenance in Chandon and Floris.
Hardscape installs and patio re-levels in River Creek, Lansdowne, and the historic district. Long-haul maintenance for property managers along Edwards Ferry Rd.
Landscape design and full-yard refreshes in Brambleton, Belmont Country Club, and Loudoun Valley. Irrigation work for the newer Goose Creek-side builds.
Mulch + bed cleanups and shrub shaping for HOA-managed properties near Lake Anne and South Lakes. Snow plow runs along Sunset Hills.
Larger wooded lots near Great Falls Village, River Bend, and Georgetown Pike. Drainage, deer pressure, long driveways, and freeze-thaw movement matter on mulch beds, patios, and stone walkways.

hero.subhead: Lawn care, hardscape, design — one Sterling crew, one phone number, nine years in the neighborhood.
hero.primary_cta_micro: Senior crew lead, real number, ~1-business-day response.

services.title: Lawn Care & Maintenance
services.blurb: Weekly mowing, edging, trimming, and weed control. Striped lines, sharp borders, no torn-up sod.

services.title: Mulch, Beds & Planting
services.blurb: Fresh mulch installed clean. Beds reshaped, plants chosen for NoVA seasons, gardens that look right in May and November.

services.title: Hardscape & Patios
services.blurb: Paver patios, walkways, and stone work that stay level for years — built once, the right way, by a crew that has been doing it for nine seasons.

services.title: Tree & Shrub Care
services.blurb: Sculpted shrubs, healthy trees, clean lines. Pruning, shaping, removal — done by a crew who knows what each species wants.

services.title: Irrigation Systems
services.blurb: Install new sprinkler systems or fix the one that's been spraying the driveway since 2019. Seasonal start-up and shutdown too.

services.title: Snow Plowing & Water Features
services.blurb: Same crew, four seasons. Winter snow + ice clearing for driveways and walkways. Spring through fall: water features and decorative installs.

process.title: Free on-site estimate
process.description: Call or fill out the form. A senior crew lead walks the property within a few days, listens to what you want, and gives you a real number — not a guess.

process.title: Design + scope approval
process.description: For installs and hardscape we sketch the plan, talk material choices, and lock the timeline. For maintenance, we agree on a schedule and start the next week.

process.title: Install + walk-through
process.description: Crew shows up when we said we'd show up. Job gets done. We walk it with you before we leave — anything off, we fix it the same day.

reviews_featured.author: Matt Tengs
reviews_featured.text: Fantastic from start to finish. KVA installed a backyard paver patio with two walkways attached. They also removed old sod and planted new grass seed in the front and back of my home. The quality and attention to detail was second to none.

reviews_featured.author: Francisco Ruben
reviews_featured.text: They had to re-level an existing walkway and two back patios. They did an excellent job. After they were done we were surprised because we had not remembered how good the walkway and patios looked when they were clean and level! I would recommend them to anyone.

reviews_featured.author: Jacob Jenkins
reviews_featured.text: We had a great experience with KVA. Not only did they provide a great end-product, they offered suggestions along the way that indicated they weren't just trying to finish the job, but they wanted the best look for our landscaping.

reviews_featured.author: Jay North
reviews_featured.text: I recently hired KVA for plant trimming and mulch installation. These guys did a bang-up job. I was super impressed with the quality of mulch and the professional installation. Nice guys too.

reviews_featured.author: Verified Thumbtack customer
reviews_featured.text: They provided superior quality work at a fair price. The crew were diligent workers and the result speaks for itself.

reviews_featured.author: Marc Misleh
reviews_featured.text: Great communication. Quality work. Give them a try, you won't be disappointed.

about.headline: One Sterling crew. Nine years. Every yard treated like a neighbor's.
about.body: KVA Landscaping is a family-run crew out of Sterling, VA. We have been doing landscape work in Loudoun and Fairfax for nine years — the same crew, the same number, the same standards. From weekly mowing and seasonal mulch to full hardscape builds, we cover the whole yard with one team that already knows your property. Most of our work is for homeowners within ten miles of Sterling — Herndon, Leesburg, Ashburn, Reston — plus a handful of property managers we have been with for years. We show up when we said we would, we do good work, and we come back when you call.
about.body_extra: Nine seasons in NoVA teach you things you can't shortcut. We know which sub-developments sit on heavy Loudoun clay (and how to amend beds so plantings actually take), which HOAs require pre-approval for paver work and how to get it through fast, and which freeze-thaw windows to schedule hardscape around so a patio still sits level five winters later. None of that ends up on a project bid — it just shows up in the way your yard holds up.

faqs.q: How fast do you respond to estimate requests?
faqs.a: Usually within an hour during business hours (Mon–Sat 8 AM – 6 PM). A senior crew lead — not a call-center — texts or calls back. After hours, you'll hear from us first thing the next business morning.

About ten miles around Sterling: Sterling, Herndon, Leesburg, Ashburn, Reston, and parts of Great Falls. The crew lives nearby, so we're not driving in from Manassas. For large hardscape projects we'll travel further — ask.
Yes, seasonal — driveways, walkways, and ice melt application for residential and a handful of small commercial properties. Sign up by October to get on the route for the coming winter.

faqs.q: What does a paver patio cost?
faqs.a: It depends — size, materials, drainage, whether we're tying into an existing walkway, whether the site needs grading. A typical residential 200–400 sq ft paver patio in NoVA falls in the $8K–$22K range. We give you a real number after walking the property, not a guess over the phone.

faqs.q: How often do you do lawn maintenance?
faqs.a: Weekly is the default in growing season, bi-weekly works for some smaller yards or cooler stretches. Each cut includes edging and string-trimming. Spring and fall cleanups (leaf removal, bed prep) we schedule separately.

Apple Pay, cash, check, credit card, PayPal, Venmo, or Zelle. For larger hardscape jobs we typically take a deposit at scope approval and the balance on walk-through, but we'll structure it however works for you.
Yes, fully licensed in Virginia and insured. Happy to share certificates of insurance for HOA-managed properties or property managers who need them on file.

estimate_form.headline: Get a free, no-pressure estimate.
estimate_form.subhead: Tell us what you need and we will be in touch within a business day — usually faster. Sterling, Herndon, Leesburg, Ashburn, Reston, and around.
